_schema.description: Annotation data for an illustration diagram. Each entry defines a callout dot position, its label, and explanatory body text. Positions are expressed as fractions (0–1) of the SVG viewBox dimensions.

fields.id: string — unique identifier, used to link dot to info panel
fields.label: string — short structure name shown as the callout label
fields.cx: number (0–1) — horizontal position of callout dot as fraction of viewBox width
fields.cy: number (0–1) — vertical position of callout dot as fraction of viewBox height
fields.lx: number (0–1) — horizontal anchor of the leader-line label
fields.ly: number (0–1) — vertical anchor of the leader-line label
fields.side: string — 'left' or 'right': which side of lx the label text extends toward
fields.color: string — hex color for this callout (use palette role colors)
fields.body: string — 1–3 sentence explanation shown in the info panel on click

_schema.replace: Replace each annotation entry with your subject's structures. Keep cx/cy as fractions matching where each structure appears in your illustration. Swap 'color' values using the hai palette: p1=#1B1B1B p2=#5C3317 p3=#B52C2C p5=#4A4D4F p6=#797C7F.

annotations.label: Right Atrium
annotations.body: Receives deoxygenated blood returning from the body via the superior and inferior vena cava. Thin-walled, low pressure.

annotations.label: Right Ventricle
annotations.body: Pumps deoxygenated blood to the lungs via the pulmonary artery. Thinner walls than the left ventricle — pulmonary circuit is low resistance.

annotations.label: Left Atrium
annotations.body: Receives oxygenated blood from the four pulmonary veins. Thicker walls than the right atrium due to slightly higher pressure.

annotations.label: Left Ventricle
annotations.body: The primary pump of the body. Walls are 3x thicker than the right ventricle — systemic circulation requires high pressure to reach the extremities.

annotations.label: Interventricular Septum
annotations.body: Muscular wall separating the two ventricles. Defects here are the most common congenital heart abnormality.

annotations.label: Aortic Valve
annotations.body: Guards the exit from the left ventricle into the aorta. Opens on systole, snaps shut on diastole to prevent backflow.

annotations.label: Pulmonary Valve
annotations.body: Guards the exit from the right ventricle into the pulmonary artery. Failure causes pulmonary regurgitation.

annotations.label: Mitral Valve
annotations.body: Two-leaflet valve between left atrium and left ventricle. The most commonly diseased heart valve — affects 2% of the global population.

annotations.label: Tricuspid Valve
annotations.body: Three-leaflet valve between right atrium and right ventricle. Chordae tendineae prevent leaflet prolapse.

annotations.label: Aorta
annotations.body: Largest artery in the body. Carries oxygenated blood from the left ventricle to systemic circulation. Diameter ~2.5 cm at the root.

annotations.label: Pulmonary Artery
annotations.body: Carries deoxygenated blood from right ventricle to lungs. Uniquely, an artery carrying venous blood — named for direction of travel, not oxygen content.

annotations.label: Superior Vena Cava
annotations.body: Returns deoxygenated blood from the upper body to the right atrium. Completes the systemic venous return along with the inferior vena cava.
